content.text:
<user_query>
Run sync orchestration for newly created toolbox assets.

Changed files:
- /Users/HanHu/.cursor/commands/checkout-for-huh8.md (new)
- /Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/checkout-for-huh8/SKILL.md (new)

Provide policy/security review and apply sync to approved destinations only.

Host-purpose context:
- local: primary local development environment; full sync peer.
- huh.desktop.us: office desktop environment for day-to-day coding and command/skill usage.
- isaacgym: remote/containerized environment for motion RL workflows; may need command/skill availability.
- Huh8.remote_kernel.fuyao: remote kernel environment for deploy/training operations; command/skill consistency desired.

Requirements:
1) Run per-host RemotePolicyReviewer and SecurityReviewer in parallel.
2) Respect gating outcomes (apply/skip/prompt).
3) If prompts are required, return concise prompt requirements.
4) If approved, execute sync apply async for approved hosts.
5) Return concise final status including counts (identical/partial/conflicts/unreachable) and destinations synced.
</user_query>

Running the sync orchestration now. I’ll first load the relevant sync skill instructions, then execute per-host policy and security reviews in parallel and gate destinations before applying async sync.

I’ve loaded the sync skill and will now run the per-host policy reviewers and security reviewers, then gate approvals before any sync command runs.

**Planning parallel tool calls**

I need to run reviewers in parallel, possibly using the multi_tool_use.parallel method, calling Subagent eight and limiting it to 4 at a time. The plan is to do two calls: first with 4 remote reviewers and then another 4 for security, all running simultaneously. I also want to include the changed files and host context. It seems like using readonly set to true is a good idea since the reviewers should only read, so let’s proceed!
